events.text: {"decision":"fail","risk":"high","work_stage":"rough-complete","component_conditions":["installed-defective","non-compliant"],"percent_complete":80,"value":0,"sound_source":"","confidence":0.9,"event_time":0,"rate":0,"order":[],"workable":[],"findings":["DP impulse lines sloped incorrectly for service: gas taps below pipe and liquid taps on top, causing condensate/air trapping and erroneous measurement","Impulse tubing arrangement does not meet API RP 551 slope requirements for gas/liquid service"],"actions":["Reconfigure impulse lines to correct slope: for gas service slope down from tap to transmitter; for liquid service slope up from tap to transmitter with gas vent at high point","Verify tubing runs per engineered drawings and manufacturer installation requirements","Re-check slope and support before closeout/hydrotest"],"rationale":"API RP 551 requires impulse piping sloped to keep condensate/air from affecting DP measurement; gas service taps must be above pipe and slope down to transmitter, liquid service taps below pipe and slope up. The observed reversed arrangement will trap liquid in gas service or air in liquid service, producing erroneous readings and potential process upsets.","references":["API RP 551","Manufacturer installation manuals"]}
